summary: WRAP is the book's biggest live risk: a post-48%-pump small-cap now fading (-4.2% today, Lorentzian -0.5/0.5 bearish) whose position grew to 41.13sh even though the bot's own critic vetoed further WRAP buys three separate times today for dilution-history/pump-signature reasons -- and the weekly buy budget is already blown (9 buys against a 4-buy cap), pointing to an overtrading/discipline gap across the whole book, not just one ticker.

WRAP.note: Entered 7/10 at $2.41 chasing a +48% weekly pop (ATF BolaWrap reclassification + WrapShield/Frenel launch); stock has since faded (-4.2% today, giving back most of the pop) and avg cost has been walked down to $2.08 (41.13sh, essentially averaging into weakness). No active S-3/424B5 shelf or ATM found in the current SEC/StockTitan filing feed (only an 8-K on the Frenel investment and clean insider Form 4 buys), so no confirmed dilution mechanism right now -- but the bot's own critic vetoed 3 further WRAP buy attempts today citing dilution-history/pump-signature failures, and the position grew anyway. Lorentzian reads -0.5 (consistency 0.5), a moderate bearish technical read consistent with the fade.

UUUU.note: Unchanged from last check: active $300M ATM (reinstated 2025-06-13) is a real ongoing dilution mechanism for this size producer; entry thesis is CEO Bhappu's $968k buy (7/7) and chairman Hansen's buy (7/8), but Lorentzian k-NN still reads -0.75 (consistency 0.75), a strong bearish technical signal the insider-buy thesis doesn't address.

CAI.note: Fundamentals still clean (director $990k buy 7/13, Caris Detect commercial launch, $100M buyback, no new dilution filing). Nightly data_quality.json (08:18Z) flags CAI as broker_disagrees on this HELD ticker (broker $17.50 vs cache $16.99, 3.0% drift) -- broker is account of record so live P&L should be fine, but any stop math referencing the cache would be off; unresolved since last check, re-flagging.

NTSK.note: Entry catalyst (fresh 52-wk high, Wells Fargo/Jefferies/Cantor Buy ratings) still real and position is +3.83 pnl, but the March-2026 IPO lockup has produced ~$18M of trailing-90-day insider selling (Lightspeed Venture Partners sold 10,621 sh on 7/8, two days before this buy) -- unaddressed by today's desk notes. Separately, today's intel.json flags a data-quality mismatch: prior materials cited a 52-wk high of $21.69 but the actual 52-wk high is $27.99 (current trade $12.41-13.69, matches broker's $13.63) -- a calibration issue worth a human check, not a price risk itself.

YEXT.note: No shelf/ATM/dilution filing found. Failed take-private bid (Feb 2026) was replaced by an anti-dilutive self-tender buyback (now $140M cap); no fresh negative catalyst.

flags.why: Still a live wildcard-sleeve position on the ledger (18.28 sh) that broker reconciliation continues to show as ledger-only (broker no longer reports it) -- unresolved for at least two cycles now. DVLT is already this desk's own avoid list as a serial diluter (855M shares out, +1518% YoY after a $60M raise, ~$2B structured-financing proposal pending); if it's still held anywhere its P/L and any stop are computed off an unconfirmed ledger price. Needs a human check before the next cycle.

Distressed reverse-merger pop, not organic strength: definitive Serapha Bio merger plus $230M concurrent private placement leaves pre-merger holders only ~3.7% of the combined entity; oncology pipeline halted, ~75% workforce cut. Unchanged, still on today's scan (score 2.06, already carrying a -3.0 internal risk penalty) and still on the desk's avoid list.

10-Q discloses going-concern doubt including possible Chapter 7 liquidation; just closed a $23.7M private placement (warrant-overhang setup). Still on today's scan (score 0.1, carrying a -3.0 internal risk penalty). FinBERT sentiment negative on the cash-runway story; no confirmable fresh positive catalyst.

Top-scoring scan candidate today (4.46, still below the 6.25 buy threshold) but carries severe dilution overhang: 167M shares outstanding, an active $300M shelf, 13.95M warrants, and authorized shares just hiked to 450M. FDA/Phase 2 news is genuinely constructive but the capital-structure risk is unresolved.

On today's scan (3.29) but distressed: down ~91% YoY near 52-wk lows despite a token insider buy, underwent a 10-for-1 reverse split this cycle tied to Nasdaq compliance, and is oddly pivoting into Dogecoin mining hardware -- a distress signal, not a turnaround catalyst.
Live $100M ATM (since 2024) plus a Feb-2026 $107.6M underwritten offering (20M sh at $5.00), now underwater near $3.73-3.83. No new filing this cycle; not held, not a current scan candidate.

Automatic S-3ASR shelf effective 1/13/26, already used twice via 424B5, plus an active $50M ATM -- ongoing drip-dilution setup. On today's scan carrying a -1.5 internal risk penalty, well below buy threshold (1.83).

dilution_watch.note: $300M at-the-market equity distribution agreement, actively usable now for this size producer.
$300M shelf plus 13.95M warrants and authorized shares hiked to 450M -- severe overhang, top of today's scan.
$107.6M underwritten offering now underwater; $100M ATM remains active.
Automatic shelf effective, used twice via 424B5; $50M ATM active.
Definitive Serapha Bio merger plus $230M concurrent private placement; existing holders diluted to ~3.7% pro forma.
$23.7M private placement; going-concern disclosure in most recent 10-Q.

disagreements: intel.json's WRAP alert reads 'not held/not on watchlist' but broker_state.json (14:14:03Z, after intel's 14:05Z snapshot) shows WRAP as a live 41.13-share position -- intel's framing undersells the real story, which is that the position grew despite the bot's own critic vetoing 3 further WRAP buys today for dilution-history/pump-signature reasons.

NTSK's post-lockup insider selling (~$18M trailing 90 days, Lightspeed sold 7/8) remains unaddressed in this cycle's intel.json and research_journal notes, which continue to read NTSK as clean on 'no negative news' -- second-order risk still live.

Neither intel.json nor today's research_journal entry mentions the weekly buy budget is already over cap (9 buys used against a 4-buy weekly limit per radar.html's execution checklist) -- worth surfacing as a standalone discipline flag, not just a per-ticker risk.
